name: Deneubourg-style reinforced random walk reference

source.primary_model: Deneubourg et al. 1990, The self-organizing exploratory pattern of the Argentine ant
source.statistical_reference: Le Goff & Soulier 2014/2016, Parameter estimation of a two-colored urn model class, arXiv:1405.2322

notes: This target encodes a literature model curve, not digitized raw ant counts.
Use it to tune qualitative symmetry breaking and branch-choice sensitivity.
Do not claim quantitative biological calibration until raw published or lab branch-choice data are supplied.

equal_branch_selected_balance.description: Fraction of runs selecting upper branch under symmetric conditions should remain near 0.5 across many seeds.

equal_branch_mean_dominance.description: Symmetric double bridge should show nonzero but variable dominance from self-reinforcement.

upper_bias_selection_rate.description: A connected initial upper-branch trail should strongly increase upper-branch selection.

upper_bias_mean_dominance.description: Biased runs should show stronger branch dominance than symmetric runs.

biased_vs_equal_dominance_gain.description: Connected trail bias should increase dominance over equal-branch baseline.
